Overview

Address
4305 S Poplar, Casper, WY 82601
Phone
3072372561
Certified beds
120
Avg daily residents
84 (70% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1984-10-14
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.00 3.68 3.15 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.60 0.64 0.63 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.41 0.82 0.43 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.98 2.22 2.09 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.01 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.10 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.59
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.39
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.72
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
55.4%
annual
RN turnover
52.9%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.30
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-06-13 30 9 23 212 1 212
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-03-23 17 6 11 96 1 96
Total weighted health score
183.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (56)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
52 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-12-30 2026-01-26
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. E 2025-08-27 2025-10-05
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-08-27 2025-10-03
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. B 2025-08-27 2025-10-10
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-08-27 2025-10-10
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. E 2025-08-27 2025-10-03
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-08-27 2025-10-03
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2025-08-27 2025-10-03
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-08-27 2025-10-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-08-27 2025-10-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-08-27 2025-10-10
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2025-08-27 2025-10-17
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. E 2025-08-27 2025-10-16
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2025-05-16 2025-06-30
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-30
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-04-18 2025-07-29
0563 Honor the resident's right to receive visitors of his or her choosing, at the time of his or her choosing. D 2025-03-06 2025-04-11
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-03-06 2025-04-11
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-06 2025-04-11
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-03-06 2025-04-11
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-01-15 2025-04-11
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0837 Establish a governing body that is legally responsible for establishing and implementing policies for managing and operating the facility and appoints a properly licensed administrator responsible for managing the facility. F 2024-06-13 2024-07-08
0839 Employ staff that are licensed, certified, or registered in accordance with state laws. E 2024-05-02 2024-05-22
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-03-21 2024-05-03
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-03-21 2024-05-03
0563 Honor the resident's right to receive visitors of his or her choosing, at the time of his or her choosing. D 2023-08-03 2023-08-23
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-08-03 2023-08-23
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-06-21 2023-06-26
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-04-14 2023-04-18
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-04-14 2023-04-18
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. E 2023-03-23 2023-03-23
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. E 2023-03-23 2023-03-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2023-03-23 2023-03-23
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2023-03-23 2023-03-23
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-03-23 2023-03-23
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2023-03-23 2023-03-23
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2023-03-23 2023-03-23
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2021-12-17 2022-01-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2021-12-17 2022-01-19
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2021-12-17 2022-01-19

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.

Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description
Deficiencies 0756 — scope_severity D 0756.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0756 — survey_date 2025-08-27 0756.survey_date
Deficiencies 0756 — tag 0756 0756.tag
Deficiencies 0757 — correction_date 2025-04-11 0757.correction_date
Deficiencies 0757 — description Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. 0757.description
Deficiencies 0757 — scope_severity D 0757.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0757 — survey_date 2025-03-06 0757.survey_date
Deficiencies 0757 — tag 0757 0757.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2023-03-23 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity E 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2023-03-23 0758.survey_date
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0759 — correction_date 2022-01-19 0759.correction_date
Deficiencies 0759 — description Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. 0759.description
Deficiencies 0759 — scope_severity D 0759.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0759 — survey_date 2021-12-17 0759.survey_date
Deficiencies 0759 — tag 0759 0759.tag

Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2023-03-23 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description

Deficiencies 0803 — description Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. 0803.description
